name: Nerets Loop
name_original: Alto de Roc de Neret desde Tremp — circular por la Presa de Sant Antoni
description: Hike the moderate 6.4-mile (10.4 km) Nerets Loop in Conca de Tremp, gaining 1640 feet (500 metres) with views of Sant Antoni Lake.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Talarn offer diverse landscapes in the Lleida province of Catalonia. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Noguera Pallaresa river, the Sant Antoni reservoir, and surrounding hills. The terrain includes riverine paths, agricultural areas, and routes leading through Mediterranean scrubland. Best hiking trails around Talarn The most popular hiking route is From Tremp to Talarn — circular along the Ribera de la Noguera Pallaresa, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Ribera de la Noguera Pallaresa, offering views of the river and surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Nerets Loop, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.4 km) path. This route features varied terrain with a significant elevation gain, providing panoramic views of the Talarn area. Local hikers also love the From Talarn to the Seròs Ravine loop from Estació de Tremp, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through the Seròs Ravine, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Talarn is defined by river valleys, reservoir views, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Talarn are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Talarn's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Talarn?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Talarn, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 15 easy routes, 26 moderate paths, and 12 more challenging trails to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in Talarn?
A: Yes, Talarn offers 15 easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219706553/geobike-xl-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geobike XL Route&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.7 km path that takes just over an hour to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's scenery.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Talarn?
A: The hiking trails around Talarn offer views of the Noguera Pallaresa river and the Sant Antoni reservoir. You can also discover specific landmarks like the natural spring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2438181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la O&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salàs de Pallars Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talarn Railway Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is another notable point of interest along some routes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Talarn area?
A: Yes, many routes in Talarn are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31849256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lo Búnker Trail – Sant Antoni Dam loop from Tremp&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.7 km circular hike that offers scenic views of the Sant Antoni Dam.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Talarn?
A: Talarn's Mediterranean climate makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are mild but some higher elevation paths might be cooler.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Talarn?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Talarn region. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in agricultural areas or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Talarn?
A: The hiking trails in Talarn are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, including river valleys, reservoir views, and rolling hills, as well as the well-maintained paths suitable for various abilities.

Q: Are there any challenging hiking routes for experienced hikers in Talarn?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Talarn offers 12 difficult routes. These trails typically feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain, providing panoramic views and a more strenuous workout. Be sure to check the route details for specific difficulty levels and prepare accordingly.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Talarn?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of Talarn, as well as at common starting points for popular trails. Many routes, such as those starting from Tremp or near the Sant Antoni Dam, have designated parking areas. It's recommended to check the specific tour details on komoot for parking information related to your chosen route.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails in Talarn?
A: The region is characterized by its proximity to the Noguera Pallaresa river and the Sant Antoni reservoir. While specific designated wild swimming spots aren't always marked, the reservoir and river offer potential opportunities for a refreshing dip, especially during warmer months. Always exercise caution and respect local regulations regarding swimming.

Q: How long do hiking routes in Talarn typically take to complete?
A: Hiking durations in Talarn vary widely depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219706553/geobike-xl-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geobike XL Route&lt;/a&gt;, can take just over an hour. Moderate trails might range from 2 to 3 hours, while more challenging routes can extend to several hours or a full day. Always check the estimated duration for your chosen trail.

Q: Can I access Talarn's hiking trails using public transport?
A: Talarn is accessible by public transport, primarily by bus services connecting it to larger towns like Tremp. From Tremp, some hiking trailheads may be within walking distance or require a short local connection. It's advisable to check current bus schedules and route starting points to plan your journey effectively.
tips: Climb the paths of the Sierra de Coll to reach the top and get an incredible view of Lake Sant Antoni.
View of the hydroelectric dam on Lake San Antoni. The natural space surrounding the lake is a great place for hiking adventures.

faqs.question: Where does the Nerets Loop trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The Nerets Loop is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends at the same point. While specific parking details are not provided, it's common for trails in the Conca de Tremp area to have designated parking near the trailhead or in nearby villages. We recommend checking the komoot tour page for the most up-to-date information on trailhead access and parking options.

faqs.question: Is the Nerets Loop suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it's a loop, it involves an elevation gain of nearly 500 meters over 10.3 kilometers, which might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. Hikers with some experience and a reasonable fitness level should find it enjoyable. Always consider the fitness level of everyone in your group before setting out.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several points of interest. You'll get a fantastic View of Sant Antoni Lake from Roc de Neret Summit, providing panoramic vistas. You will also pass by the impressive Sant Antoni Dam, a significant man-made monument in the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Conca de Tremp, the best times to hike this trail are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the Nerets Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region, but it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and for the safety of other hikers. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog, especially on warmer days, as natural water sources might be limited or seasonal.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Conca de Tremp area?
faqs.answer: As a komoot Smarttour, the Nerets Loop itself does not require a specific permit or entrance fee. The trail is located within the broader Conca de Tremp area, which is not a national park with controlled access. However, always be mindful of local regulations, especially concerning fire restrictions or protected natural areas, which might have specific rules.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Nerets Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is categorized as moderate, suggesting varied terrain. You can expect a mix of paths, possibly including rocky sections, dirt tracks, and some ascents and descents, given the nearly 500 meters of elevation gain and loss. Sturdy hiking shoes are recommended.

faqs.question: Does the Nerets Loop intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails in the area. You will find yourself on parts of paths such as Diblant lo fang, Geobike XL, Slick Rock, and Megatampanada 2023, among others. This offers a glimpse into the wider network of trails available in Conca de Tremp.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Nerets Loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this hike is approximately 3 hours and 25 minutes. This estimate is based on an average hiking pace and does not include extended stops for breaks, photography, or enjoying the viewpoints.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Nerets Loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend packing essentials such as sufficient water, snacks, appropriate footwear (hiking boots or sturdy trail shoes), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A basic first-aid kit and a fully charged phone are also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the Nerets Loop for refreshments?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural outdoor experience, the Nerets Loop is located near Tremp, a town in Conca de Tremp. You will likely find various cafes, restaurants, and pubs in Tremp or other nearby villages where you can enjoy refreshments or a meal before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: View of Sant Antoni Lake from Roc de Neret Summit
